node_location.description: Name of the Google Distributed Cloud Edge zone where this node pool will be created. For example: `us-central1-edge-customer-a`.
node_location.rationale: Node location determines the physical and geographical placement of nodes, impacting data residency, latency, and compliance with regional regulations.

node_count.description: The number of nodes in the pool.
node_count.rationale: Proper node count ensures adequate capacity for workloads while avoiding resource overprovisioning that could increase attack surface.
node_count.non-compliant: 1 (single point of failure) or excessive nodes beyond requirements

name.description: The resource name of the node pool.
name.rationale: Node pool name is an identifier and doesn't directly impact security.

location.rationale: Location selection impacts data residency, compliance with regional regulations, and disaster recovery capabilities.

cluster.description: The name of the target Distributed Cloud Edge Cluster.
cluster.rationale: Cluster association ensures nodes are deployed to the correct, authorized cluster with proper security controls.

labels.description: Labels associated with this resource. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.
labels.rationale: Proper labeling ensures node pools can be properly identified, tracked, and managed according to organizational policies and security requirements.
labels.compliant: Contains required labels: environment, team, cost-center
labels.non-compliant: Missing required labels or using non-standard label names

machine_filter.description: Only machines matching this filter will be allowed to join the node pool. The filtering language accepts strings like "name=<name>", and is documented in more detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).
machine_filter.rationale: Machine filters ensure nodes run only on approved, properly configured hardware with adequate security controls and compliance certifications.
machine_filter.non-compliant: Overly permissive filters or missing filters allowing unapproved hardware

local_disk_encryption.description: Local disk encryption options. This field is only used when enabling CMEK support.
local_disk_encryption.rationale: Local disk encryption protects sensitive data at rest on node storage and should use customer-managed keys for enhanced security control.
local_disk_encryption.non-compliant: Missing encryption or using default Google-managed keys when CMEK is required

kms_key.description: The Cloud KMS CryptoKey e.g. projects/{project}/locations/{location}/keyRings/{keyRing}/cryptoKeys/{cryptoKey} to use for protecting node local disks. If not specified, a Google-managed key will be used instead.
kms_key.rationale: Customer-managed keys provide better control over encryption policies, access controls, and key rotation procedures.
kms_key.non-compliant: Missing or improperly formatted KMS key reference

kms_key_active_version.description: (Output) The Cloud KMS CryptoKeyVersion currently in use for protecting node local disks. Only applicable if kmsKey is set.
kms_key_active_version.rationale: Output-only field indicating current key version, no security impact.

kms_key_state.description: (Output) Availability of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey. If not KEY_AVAILABLE, then nodes may go offline as they cannot access their local data. This can be caused by a lack of permissions to use the key, or if the key is disabled or deleted.
kms_key_state.rationale: Output-only field indicating key status, no security impact.

node_config.description: Configuration for each node in the NodePool.
node_config.rationale: Node configuration impacts security posture, resource allocation, and isolation between workloads.
node_config.compliant: Properly configured node settings with security labels

labels.rationale: Node labels enable proper workload placement, security zoning, and resource management based on security requirements.
labels.non-compliant: Missing security labels or incorrect labeling that could lead to improper workload placement

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
project.rationale: Project selection impacts resource isolation, billing accountability, and access control boundaries.
